seoTitle: How to Integrate Typeform With WordPress
metaDescription: Connect Typeform to WordPress and add interactive forms to your pages using the block editor, custom HTML, or a plugin.
intro: Interactive forms can boost engagement on a WordPress site, whether for feedback, sign-ups, or lead generation. This guide shows how to integrate Typeform with WordPress by generating an embed code and placing it on your pages using the block editor, custom HTML, or a plugin.

steps.heading: Open Typeform share options
steps.body: Go to the share settings of the Typeform you want to add to your site to access embedding options.

steps.heading: Copy the embed code
steps.body: Generate and copy the embed code Typeform provides for adding the form to an external website like WordPress.

steps.heading: Add Typeform to WordPress
steps.body: Paste the embed code into a custom HTML block in the WordPress editor, or use a plugin designed for embedding forms.

steps.heading: Customize and test the integration
steps.body: Adjust the form's appearance to match your site's branding, then preview the page to confirm it displays and functions correctly.

faq.question: Can I use Typeform for newsletter signups on WordPress?
faq.answer: Yes, Typeform is commonly used for newsletter signups, registrations, and lead generation forms embedded directly on WordPress pages.

faq.question: Does embedding Typeform slow down my WordPress site?
faq.answer: Embedded forms load content from Typeform's servers, so performance impact is generally minimal, but it's worth testing your page's load time after adding it.

faq.question: Can I customize the look of the embedded form?
faq.answer: Yes, you can adjust the form's theme and branding in Typeform before embedding so it better matches your WordPress site's design.

faq.question: Is coding knowledge required to integrate Typeform?
faq.answer: Not necessarily, since the WordPress block editor and available plugins are designed to handle the embed code without deep coding knowledge.
